# Project Guide (generated by orchestrator)

This directory is the **target web service** built by the multi-agent orchestrator.
Every role agent follows the conventions here. (Auto-loaded by Claude-family backends.)

## Tech stack
{{STACK}}

(The architect may override this per the spec; record the rationale in `docs/design/` when changed.)

## Directory layout
- `docs/design/` — architecture, API contract, data model
- `docs/test/` — E2E test sheets
- `backend/` — server/API code
- `frontend/` — client code
- `db/` — schema & migrations
- `tests/` — automated tests
- `.orchestrator/` — run state (board/events/results). **Do not edit**; excluded from VCS.

## Result-file coordination protocol (important)
- Edit only files inside this directory (cwd). Never access paths outside it.
- When done, write your result to the assigned `.orchestrator/results/<role>__<unit>.json`:
  ```json
  {"status": "...", "artifacts": ["relative/path"], "notes": ["..."], "blockers": [], "units": []}
  ```
- The board (`.orchestrator/board.json`) is written only by the orchestrator. You read it; you never write it.
- Do not touch other work units' files. If a shared change is needed, report it via `blockers`.

## Coding conventions
- Small, clear changes; match existing code style.
- Secrets via env vars/secrets only; no plaintext secrets.
- Commit only runnable code. Record how to run/test in `notes`.

## Cost & environment discipline
- Do NOT create virtualenvs, install dependencies (pip/npm install), download packages, build
  production bundles, or start long-running servers — CI handles install/build, and only QA runs tests.
- Write and edit source files; keep output focused. Avoid redundant scaffolding.
